payload.summary: Verified all five amendments against the source code. The backoff bug (fr-14-5f0246bf) is a confirmed critical defect: active_run is set to None at line 4636, and after the sleep the loop continues to line 4473 where carry_forward_active_run calls current_active_run, which returns CorruptRecord when active_run is None. The test coverage amendment (fr-14-79b4ebc2) targets this confirmed-buggy, untested code path. The three specification-level amendments (fr-14-96b90f99, fr-14-d8929c4f, fr-14-0878206f) all identify real inconsistencies between the bead specification and the actual code constraints.

votes.rationale: Confirmed by reading the source. At engine.rs:4634 preserve_interrupted_run(snapshot) copies active_run to interrupted_run, then line 4636 sets snapshot.active_run = None. After the tokio::select! sleep at lines 4661-4664, the `continue` at line 4686 loops back to line 4473 where carry_forward_active_run(snapshot, ...) is called. This function calls current_active_run(snapshot) at line 590, which at line 559-567 returns Err(AppError::CorruptRecord) when active_run is None. This is a guaranteed fatal error on every retry with non-zero backoff — the interrupted_run is preserved but never restored. The proposed fix (restore active_run from interrupted_run and reset status to Running after sleep, before continue) is the correct resolution.

Given the confirmed bug in fr-14-5f0246bf, this code path is both critical and demonstrably broken without any test catching it. The backoff snapshot code at lines 4622-4664 is the only place that sets active_run = None mid-retry-loop, making it uniquely fragile. The three proposed verification points — (1) snapshot state during backoff, (2) successful retry after restore, (3) resume recovery from killed-during-backoff — precisely target the failure modes this code introduces. Test coverage here would have caught the CorruptRecord bug before it was merged.

Verified in source: DEFAULT_MIN_REVIEWERS is 2 (config.rs:25), default_final_review_backends() returns two PanelBackendSpec::required entries (config.rs:1473-1477), and the skip logic at final_review.rs:227 and 429 only triggers when `!member.required`. With both backends marked required, any backend failure immediately returns Err at line 235/437 — the skip path is unreachable. The bead's acceptance criterion to proceed with one reviewer is therefore impossible under current defaults without also changing required-member handling or min_reviewers. The specification needs to be internally consistent with these constraints.

Confirmed in source: completion.rs line 135 uses `.await?` which propagates any invocation error immediately with no skip-on-failure logic — there is no `if !member.required` guard as exists in final_review.rs. Similarly, prompt_review.rs line 191 uses `.await?` with the same immediate propagation pattern. Neither module has graceful backend-failure handling. If the bead claims to address backend unavailability across all multi-backend stages but only implements the fix in final_review.rs, completion and prompt_review panels will still fail fatally on the same class of persistent backend outage.

Verified in source: extract_stdout_error at process_backend.rs:1478-1487 correctly gates on is_error==true before treating stdout as an error message. Raw stdout from a successful invocation can contain arbitrary model output, including text about billing, credits, or any other topic. If the backend-unavailable detection described in the bead matches broad substrings against raw stdout/stderr without respecting the is_error gate, it would create false positives — a model legitimately discussing billing topics would be misclassified as unavailable. Detection must be scoped to structured error channels from failed invocations only.
